item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my second favourite SCP. Just got active so I have to upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this, it's fun and creepy in that way that is unique to animated puppets, and the escalating weirdness and then impossibility of its advice is a good hook. I'm only sad that we didn't get to see more of it - I usually avoid giving the "add a log" advice, but this is one case where I think a table of its advice would have been cool.

My main issue with the article is that I don't care for the frequent use of examples to make your points. It feels a little jarring and generally shouldn't be necessary to communicate the info you're trying to get across - the stuff being presented isn't complex, and was easily understandable before the example. My personal feeling is that if these example sections were removed and replaced with a log of some examples instead, this article would be much stronger.

Despite that though, I did enjoy this, so +1 from me.

Minor issue
– hide block

other than English, or who are illiterate,

This line is a little awkward for a couple of reasons. One, illiteracy would have no bearing on the ability to understand speech, so it doesn't seem like it needs to be mentioned. Two, English should not be assumed as the default (despite this being an English language site, the Foundation is global.) You could better describe the phenomena as something like "Subjects will hear this in their primary language." Unless you actually meant that it only speaks English, and will speak in English even to people who don't understand it; if that's the case, it's unclear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good! It’s simple but interesting and effective. Reminds me of a lot of series 1 skips. Great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this - it's a good example of subtle, building spookiness. To me it's not the fact that the doll kills you that's scary, it's the way it follows you around and the almost-but-not-quite graspable internal logic of the thing.
